segments.text: Hi, I'm going to talk to you today about
Sonar Cube advanced security. This is a
new offering that extends the security
capabilities that customers have come to
rely on in Sonar Cub's products. This
offering has been available for Sonar
CCube Server since May of 2025. And now
here in September, we're making this
available for Sonar Cube Cloud. It's
available for Enterprise Plus editions.
Let's get into it.
What we've done with this offering is
extend the core security capabilities
that customers love and know in Sonar
Cube to go beyond firstparty code and
the technology stack more deeply into
third party open source.
As you can see from this iceberg
illustrated here, what's above the
waterline, what your developers are
writing in house is really only about
20% of the picture of how modern
software is assembled, shipped, and
delivered to your IM customers.
What we're providing in advanced
security includes advanced SAS, which is
taint analysis that goes much deeper
down the call paths of how your
developer firstparty code is calling
thirdparty open source libraries. What
this delivers to our customers is better
visibility on risk. As an example, you
can see here we have a real world
finding where we found a 9.8 critical
CVSs scored vulnerability.
This was only available because we knew
the way that the customer was using it
down the call paths would make the code
vulnerable.
We're also including software
composition analysis or SCA. This is
critical to understand all of the open
source libraries that are in use. What
is the license of those libraries and
are there any current open
vulnerabilities?
The core SCA use cases that we're
supporting right now include
vulnerability management or the ability
to remediate and remove vulnerabilities
in your direct and transitive
dependencies.
We're providing the ability to create a
software bill of materials or esbomb.
This allows customers both to understand
their open source dependency surface
area but also have a machine readable
format that they can share that
document. Uh they can share that with
their customers or they could share it
internally with other business
processes.
We're also providing the quality gates
that customers um use in the Synarch
product. This allows you to gate on new
risk entering your codebase and to
customize that based on new code or
overall code.
And then finally, sort of a specialized
version of equality gate is the
licensing management capabilities. So
this allows our customers to configure
and enforce licensing policies, ensuring
that developers don't have to track down
your internal policies on licensing. Um,
it's right there in the workflows
they're already using.
We're do doing all of this in
partnership with open source
maintainers. We have contractual
business relationships that ensure
dependency life cycle resilience and
also give us a deeper perspective if and
when a CDE happens on a package. Um, and
they're also putting into place some
secure development practices. All of
these pieces of these contracts are
instrumented to reduce time and effort
and toil that developers are spending
managing their open source dependencies.
So now we're going to hop into a demo.
And I want to call out there's also a
link here on our website where you can
also go play around with the demo
yourself.

fullText: Hi, I'm going to talk to you today about Sonar Cube advanced security. This is a new offering that extends the security capabilities that customers have come to rely on in Sonar Cub's products. This offering has been available for Sonar CCube Server since May of 2025. And now here in September, we're making this available for Sonar Cube Cloud. It's available for Enterprise Plus editions. Let's get into it. What we've done with this offering is extend the core security capabilities that customers love and know in Sonar Cube to go beyond firstparty code and the technology stack more deeply into third party open source. As you can see from this iceberg illustrated here, what's above the waterline, what your developers are writing in house is really only about 20% of the picture of how modern software is assembled, shipped, and delivered to your IM customers. What we're providing in advanced security includes advanced SAS, which is taint analysis that goes much deeper down the call paths of how your developer firstparty code is calling thirdparty open source libraries. What this delivers to our customers is better visibility on risk. As an example, you can see here we have a real world finding where we found a 9.8 critical CVSs scored vulnerability. This was only available because we knew the way that the customer was using it down the call paths would make the code vulnerable. We're also including software composition analysis or SCA. This is critical to understand all of the open source libraries that are in use. What is the license of those libraries and are there any current open vulnerabilities? The core SCA use cases that we're supporting right now include vulnerability management or the ability to remediate and remove vulnerabilities in your direct and transitive dependencies. We're providing the ability to create a software bill of materials or esbomb. This allows customers both to understand their open source dependency surface area but also have a machine readable format that they can share that document. Uh they can share that with their customers or they could share it internally with other business processes. We're also providing the quality gates that customers um use in the Synarch product. This allows you to gate on new risk entering your codebase and to customize that based on new code or overall code. And then finally, sort of a specialized version of equality gate is the licensing management capabilities. So this allows our customers to configure and enforce licensing policies, ensuring that developers don't have to track down your internal policies on licensing. Um, it's right there in the workflows they're already using. We're do doing all of this in partnership with open source maintainers. We have contractual business relationships that ensure dependency life cycle resilience and also give us a deeper perspective if and when a CDE happens on a package. Um, and they're also putting into place some secure development practices. All of these pieces of these contracts are instrumented to reduce time and effort and toil that developers are spending managing their open source dependencies. So now we're going to hop into a demo. And I want to call out there's also a link here on our website where you can also go play around with the demo yourself.
